steps.title: Write the function
steps.content:
The phrase $21$ more than $5$ times $x$ means:
$$f(x)=5x+21$$
An $x$-intercept occurs when $f(x)=0$, so solve:
$$0=5x+21$$

steps.title: Find the intercept input
steps.content:
Subtract $21$ from both sides and divide by $5$:
$$-21=5x$$
$$x=-\frac{21}{5}$$

steps.title: Interpret the intercept
steps.content: The $x$-intercept means the number is $-\frac{21}{5}$ when $f(x)=0$. The answer is A.
